_comment: Auto-generated by scripts/sync_schemas.py from the ingext_schema repo. Do not edit by hand. Keys are KQL table names as returned by list_data_tables.

AzureAuditLogs.description: Schema for the Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) AuditLogs table. Contains system activity records for user and group management, application and directory changes, and other administrative operations. Query the table as AuditLogs. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/reference/tables/auditlogs

queries.description: Returns all directory group, role, and permission changes initiated by the specified user within the selected time range. This report helps investigators understand what actions the user performed ...

Returns all directory group, role, and permission changes where the specified user was the target within the selected time range. This report helps investigators determine whether the user's access...

AzureSigninLogs.description: Schema for the Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) SigninLogs table. Contains interactive and non-interactive user sign-in events, including authentication, conditional access, risk, device, and location details. Query the table as SigninLogs. Dynamic fields hinted as JSON (e.g. DeviceDetail, LocationDetails, Status) support dot/bracket access directly; plain String fields holding JSON text require parse_json() first. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/reference/tables/signinlogs

queries.description: Returns the sign-in activity history for the specified user within the selected time range. This report provides a detailed timeline of the user's authentication events in Azure Active Directory, h...

NetworkFortigateEvent.description: Schema for FortiGate event and UTM logs (type=event and type=utm). Fields above _fields are shared across FortiGate log families (see FortiGate Traffic for matching definitions); subtype-specific columns are held in the _fields dynamic JSON bag keyed by logid. Reference: https://docs.fortinet.com/document/fortigate/7.6.6/fortios-log-message-reference/357866/log-message-fields

NetworkFortigateTraffic.description: Schema for FortiGate firewall traffic (forward session) logs (type=traffic). One row per session. Subtype-specific keys not modeled as columns are held in the _fields dynamic JSON bag. Reference: https://docs.fortinet.com/document/fortigate/7.6.6/fortios-log-message-reference/357866/log-message-fields

Office365.description: Unified schema for Microsoft 365 audit events. Use 'applicableWorkloads' to determine if a field is valid for the queried workload.

behavior.description: Fluency behavior timeline event (notable event). Each record represents one behavior detected for an entity (the "key") by a behavior rule, along with a risk contribution and first-seen/recurrence history. Rule-specific details are carried in the attributes array and its map form, AttributeMap.

queries.description: Lists email threat-intelligence behaviors detected by the Security Compliance Center mail-data rule over the past 7 days, surfacing messages with a "Phish" verdict. The rule-specific details (verdi...

office365Application.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) application registration snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one app registration: identity, credentials (client secrets and certificates), requested API permissions, redirect/auth configuration, and publisher/governance settings. The application object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. web.implicitGrantSettings.enableIdTokenIssuance) and arrays require mv-expand. Useful for app-governance and credential-hygiene reporting (e.g. over-privileged apps, expiring secrets).

queries.description: Finds application registrations that request high-privilege Microsoft Graph (or other API) application permissions (type == "Role", i.e. app-only consent that does not require a signed-in user). Fl...

office365Device.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) device resource snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one registered/joined device: identity, OS and hardware, compliance and management status, trust/join type, on-prem sync, and registration timestamps. The device object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. extensionAttributes.extensionAttribute1) and arrays require mv-expand. Useful for device-inventory, stale-device, and compliance reporting.

queries.description: Finds enabled devices that have not signed in for more than 90 days (approximateLastSignInDateTime older than the threshold), i.e. stale device registrations that should be reviewed and cleaned up....

office365Group.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) group resource snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one group: identity, mail settings, group type and security flags, membership (and dynamic membership rule), lifecycle timestamps, and on-premises sync details. The group object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so arrays (members, proxyAddresses, groupTypes) require mv-expand. Useful for membership audits, group-inventory, and security/role-assignable group reporting.

queries.description: Lists Microsoft 365 ("Unified") groups with their member counts and key settings (visibility, mail/security flags), ordered by size. Useful for group inventory, spotting empty or oversized collabor...

office365InstalledApp.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) service principal (enterprise application) snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the tenant-local instance of an application — including first-party Microsoft apps, gallery/SaaS apps, and the service principals backing your own app registrations. This is distinct from office365Application (the app registration / global definition); office365InstalledApp is what is actually installed/consented in the tenant. The object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so arrays (servicePrincipalNames, oauth2PermissionScopes) require mv-expand. Useful for enterprise-app inventory, consent/credential review, and spotting disabled or credentialed service principals.

queries.description: Inventory of enabled enterprise applications (service principals) in the tenant, classifying each as a Microsoft first-party app or a third-party / own-tenant app (via appOwnerOrganizationId) and c...

office365User.description: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD) user resource snapshot collected by Ingext. Each record is the current state of one directory user: profile, account status, assigned licenses and service plans, group and role memberships, registered authentication methods, and MFA/SSPR registration details. The user object is stored as a dynamic JSON document, so nested objects are accessed with dot notation (e.g. userRegistration.isMfaRegistered) and arrays require mv-expand.
